pattern: Every insertion point is given in strictly increasing order, so a single left-to-right pass copying each slice of the original string and dropping a space right before each requested index builds the answer in one shot -- no shifting or re-indexing needed.
hints: Because the space positions are strictly increasing and refer to the ORIGINAL string, you never need to re-adjust indices as you insert.
Walk through the space positions in order, copying the chunk of the original string since the last position, then append a single space before continuing.
Don't forget the final chunk of the string after the last requested space position.

edge_inputs.description: space requested at the very first index
space requested right before the last character
single character string with a space before it
mixed uppercase and lowercase with scattered positions
only one space requested in a longer string

stress.description: random-length mixed-case string with a random strictly increasing subset of positions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 300000))
    letters = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(n))
    k = max(1, min(n, rng.randint(1, n)))
    spaces = sorted(rng.sample(range(n), k))
    return [s, spaces]
